title: Hieracium albopellitum
taxonomy: Hieracium albopellitum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Within this family, it is classified under the genus Hieracium, which consists of over 1000 species of plants. The genus Hieracium is often referred to as hawkweeds. Hieracium albopellitum can be further classified as a member of the subgenus Pilosella. This classification places Hieracium albopellitum within the Pilosella group of hawkweeds, known for their white or yellowish flowers.

intro: Hieracium albopellitum, also known as White Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the alleviation of symptoms associated with various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium albopellitum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to address various health concerns, including fever reduction and immune system support.
